title: Libvirt can't access /dev/kvm passed to the container with --device /dev/kvm
body:
### Issue Description

When running podman as root (but also without root) and passing `--device /dev/kvm` the libvirt inside the container is not able to access /dev/kvm device. I'm testing the access with `virt-host-validate` utility.

I have access to /dev/kvm just fine on the host and the same reproducer was tested on multiple machines where this works for others. It worked for me also for quite a while but not anymore. I'm not sure what have happened.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. sudo podman run --device /dev/kvm -it --rm fedora:41
2. dnf install -y libvirt
3. virt-host-validate


### Describe the results you received

Between the lines I'm getting find:

```
  QEMU: Checking if device '/dev/kvm' is accessible                          : FAIL (Check /dev/kvm is world writable or you are in a group that is allowed to access it)
```

### Describe the results you expected

The tool should return:

```
  QEMU: Checking if device '/dev/kvm' is accessible                          : PASS
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Running podman on Fedora 41 on Sway Atomic Desktop where I'm updating daily.

I can see the /dev/kvm in the container:
```
[root@fa99d63b541f /]# ls -lhZ /dev/kvm 
crw-rw-rw-. 1 root kvm system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c614,c949 10, 232 Nov 19 14:03 /dev/kvm
```

### Additional information

The same case is working with `--privileged`. However, it doesn't work with `--security-opts label=disable`.
I tested this also with someone else running F41 (not atomic) and it seems to be working correctly.

The same solution should work also rootless. We are using that in the team on daily basis.

comments.body: it is a regression in crun.  You need to upgrade to crun 1.18.2
That is great to know. Thank you for your help.
